page: The Microsoft Azure D64as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dasv7 server family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with multithreading enabled. It features 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The CPU includes an L3 cache of 32 MB and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on external Premium Storage options for persistent disk needs.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64as_v7 demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core tests and PassMark CPU scores. Multi-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for multi-core stress-ng and bzip3 decompression. Memory subsystem performance is robust, with read-only large block bandwidth reaching 206.82 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 128.87 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Database operations and single-threaded compression tasks also rank in the top 10%, with Redis SET operations achieving 94.83 million operations per second. For machine learning workloads, the instance achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 44.43 tokens per second on medium 7B LLM models, though performance drops to average levels for larger 70B models.

From a resource density perspective, the D64as_v7 offers a balanced hardware profile suitable for enterprise workloads requiring substantial compute power without the extreme memory overhead of memory-optimized tiers. The primary tradeoff is the complete absence of local NVMe storage, meaning all disk I/O must traverse the network to attached Premium Storage, which may introduce latency compared to local-storage-equipped instances. Given its top-tier database benchmark performance and balanced 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is appropriate for relational database hosting, static web serving at scale, multi-threaded data compression, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure D64as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dasv7 family, featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and 256.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and SHA_NI. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on external Premium Storage. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU and database performance, alongside strong multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. It achieves 94.83 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and top-tier text generation speeds for 7B LLM models. The D64as_v7 is suited for enterprise applications, web serving, database hosting, and medium-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v7 is a general-purpose instance featuring a 64-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor and 256 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier single-core and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64as_v7 features a 64-vCPU AMD EPYC processor and 256 GB RAM, delivering top-tier single-core and database benchmark performance.
tagline: A balanced sixty-four vCPU virtual machine delivering top-tier single-core execution and robust memory bandwidth for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
256.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU and database operation benchmark performance
Supports AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and cryptographic hardware acceleration
No local storage included; compatible with external Premium Storage
Suited for relational databases, web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference
